Month 2: 

Trying to land interviews with Google/Uber/AirBnb

title: Positive AA Round for Microsoft SSE (April Drive), but Recruiter has gone silent? Seeking advice.
content:
I’m looking for some clarity on Microsoft’s current hiring process, as my experience has been a bit of a rollercoaster.
Timeline:

* First week of April: Contacted for a hiring drive for SSE role.
* April 14th: Completed my final AA (As Appropriate) round for an SSE role.
* Post-Interview: After 5 days of silence, I reached out to the scheduler (my only point of contact). She confirmed that the feedback for all rounds was positive.
* April 20th: The recruiter finally emailed me asking for documents. However, there was no mention of the specific role, level, or team.
* Current Status: I’ve sent the documents and followed up multiple times asking for level details, but I’ve received zero response. No acknowledgement, no "out of office," nothing.

The Doubts:

   1. Is this ghosting or just "Big Tech" bureaucracy? It has been about 3 weeks since the AA and 2 weeks since I sent my documents. Is it normal for recruiters to go completely silent after asking for paperwork?
   2. No Level/Team Info: Is it standard for them to ask for documents before confirming the level (63/64) or the specific team? I feel like I'm flying blind.
   3. Action Center Status: My portal has been stuck in the "Interview" state for over 2 weeks now. Does this usually flip to "Completed" or "Offer" only after the background check starts?
   4. No Contact Details: Since the recruiter only uses a generic email and hasn't shared a phone number, I have no way to get a real-time update.

Has anyone else dealt with this lately? I’m worried that despite the "positive feedback" from the coordinator, the offer might be stalled or the headcount was pulled. Would love to hear from anyone who has joined Microsoft recently or is currently in the "Interview" status limbo.

title: Kotak SDE-2 Offer Evaluation | Lowballed
content:
# Backend Engineer (~3.5 YOE, 2023 grad, Tier ~1.5)

17.25 LPA Base + 1L bonus(Joining)  + ~12L ESOPs (4 yrs, private)

**Got SDE-2 offer :**
26.8 LPA Base + 2.68 variable (10%) + 3L joining
32.48 LPA (1st year)

Expected ~30+ base, but HR seems rigid about the budget , Feels like a lowballed offer. Also expecting appraisal/promotion soon here.

Should I try negotiating further or wait and continue interviewing?

What’s the best way to negotiate this offer further, especially on the base component?

Tech stack: Java , Spring , Team not known yet
Please help out !!

title: Roblox vs Rippling vs LinkedIn [Offer Eval]
content:
SDE at FAANG with 6.5 YOE, was fortunate enough to get a few offers and wanted some advice from folks working at these companies

1. Roblox [Senior - San Mateo]
Base Comp - 237K
RSU - 380K(first year ~155k)
Total Comp - 392K

2. Rippling[Senior - SF office]
Base Comp - 260K
Stock Options - 190K (first year)
Total Comp - 450K 

3. Linkedin[Senior - Sunnyvale]
Base Comp - 220K
RSU -  110K
Sign on Bonus - 40K
Total Comp - 370K

Benefits are largely the same - Standard stuff, Free lunch, 401K match, etc

I am leaning towards Roblox given the work culture and the stock price is at its lowest right now but it only seems to be going down, the team at Rippling is great they maintain the low level compute infra as well as streaming pipelines (its something I wanted to work on for a long time). 
Although I have heard terrible things about Rippling's WLB and that the engineering culture is bad. Although I imagine if I really like the work I wont mind the bad WLB.
LinkedIn is for the Apps Track and the team doesnt really sound exciting as well. 

Rippling says they are going to go public in 2029 and there is upside in both Roblox and Rippling's stocks.

title: SocGen (Senior Specialist) vs JPMC (35L Base) for 7 YOE Full Stack | Bangalore
content:

 I have 7 years of experience primarily in Java, Spring Boot, and React.
I recently secured an offer from JPMC with a 35L Base (Fixed).I’m trying to figure out if SocGen can/will match or exceed this for the Senior Specialist grade. From what I’ve gathered, 35L base seems to be at the very top (or even above) the usual band for this role at SocGen.

I’d love your suggestions on:
Salary Band: Does SocGen pay 35L+ Fixed for Senior Specialists? Or is that reserved for Lead/AVP levels?

WLB vs Pay: Is the Work-Life Balance at SocGen significantly better enough to justify a potentially lower pay compared to JPMC?

title: Understanding Salary calculation / Negotiation
content:
Hey Leetcode community,

I’m looking for guidance on how to accurately evaluate and present Total Compensation (TC), and what a realistic expectation should be for my next job switch.

Current YOE: ~6 years. Senior MTS at Oracle

TC: Base is 19LPA, (received single digit / no hikes on base at all)
My initial stock component has completely vested and got a peanut refresher that is 1/5 th of my initial offering. 
With my new vesting, and current stock price, my ESOPs would come to around ~6 LPA. Previously it was close to ~25 LPA

I have the following questions, Please help!

1) Hike Percentage:
a) What percentage hike is reasonable to target (e.g., 30%, 50%, etc.)?
b) To what extent does future TC depend on current TC?
c) How should one decide their “Expected CTC” during interviews?
d) How can we identify the median salary range for a company/role beforehand? Are platforms like levels.fyi, Glassdoor, or blind reliable for Indian compensation data?

2) Proof of ESOP Income:
a) Fixed salary can be validated via payslips, but how do candidates typically prove ESOP income? What do recruiters ask for?
b) Should only currently vesting ESOPs be included in TC? Is it acceptable (or common) to include previously vested stock to present a higher TC?

Thanks in advance!

title: Amazon offer || Need Suggestions
content:
Hi everyone,

I recently received an offer for an L4 SDE-1 role at Amazon. However, my recruiter informed me that I have two candidate accounts associated with different email IDs and advised me to delete one of them. They mentioned this could potentially create issues during onboarding if not resolved.
The reason is that I had given an interview from another account for an FTC role and got rejected within a month of my full-time interview schedule. She said it’s up to me and that I have to delete the account by any means, as she can’t help with it.
After that, she sent me the offer details, and today I completed my pre-boarding steps and also uploaded my batch photo. I have already resigned from my current company, and today I also acknowledged the offer letter from Amazon.
What will happen if they revoke my offer? I will be in a very difficult situation, as I have also rejected another offer from Marshall and currently don’t have any other offers. Do you think they might reject the offer based on this, or am I overthinking?
Also, what does she mean by saying there might be a problem during onboarding?

I've been trying to contact support through toll-free numbers and have also sent multiple emails to hr-dsr-privacy@amazon.com, but I haven't received any response so far.

This is really important for my onboarding, so I would greatly appreciate any guidance or help on how to resolve this.

Thank you in advance

title: Managing DSA, Development and Academics simultaneously
content:
Managing DSA (Data Structures and Algorithms),development, and academics simultaneously requires effective time management, prioritization, and a balanced approach. Here are some tips to help you manage your time and responsibilities:

1. Create a schedule: Plan out your day, week, and month. Set aside dedicated time for DSA practice, development projects, academic studies, and relaxation.
2. Prioritize: Focus on the most important tasks and activities. Identify critical academic deadlines, urgent development tasks, and essential DSA topics.
3. Break tasks into smaller chunks: Divide large tasks into smaller, manageable pieces. This will help you make progress in multiple areas simultaneously.
4. Use productivity tools: Utilize tools like Trello, Todoist, or Notion to organize tasks, track progress, and set reminders.
5. DSA practice: Allocate a specific time slot for DSA practice, and stick to it. Start with easier topics and gradually move to more challenging ones.
6. Development projects: Set aside dedicated time for development projects. Break down larger projects into smaller tasks to make progress.
7. Academic studies: Attend classes regularly, take notes, and review material consistently. Set aside time for assignments, projects, and studying.
8. Take breaks: Make time for relaxation, exercise, and socializing. Taking breaks will help you recharge and maintain productivity.
9. Seek help when needed: Don't hesitate to ask for help from teachers, mentors, or peers when struggling with DSA, development, or academic concepts.
10. Review and adjust: Regularly review your schedule and adjust as needed. Life is unpredictable, and your schedule may need to adapt to changing circumstances.
11. Use your commute: Utilize your daily commute to practice DSA, listen to development podcasts, or review academic material.
12. Stay organized: Keep your study materials, development projects, and DSA resources organized, both physically and digitally.

**Remember, managing multiple responsibilities requires flexibility, discipline, and patience. Stay committed, and you'll find a balance that works for you.**

title: Amazon SDE-1 Offer ||  Need help || India BLR
content:
I’m currently in a bit of a unique situation and would really value insights from this community.

I recently received an offer from Amazon for an SDE-1 role, and my onboarding process has already begun. Howeve it came to light that I had previously created another job account (with a different email) and had appeared for an interview within a short time frame. My recruiter called me and asked me to delete that profile. She warned me that there might be issue during onboarding.Even though she sent me the offer enclosed details.

My recruiter has advised that this duplicate account should be deleted, but despite multiple attempts (emails, tickets, follow-ups), I haven’t been able to get it resolved yet. I’ve also been informed that this might create issues during onboarding. Cause no one is responding and deleting my profile. There is no way you can delete your job profile, except emailing them.

I’ve been transparent about everything from my side and am actively trying to fix it, but I wanted to understand:

Has anyone faced a similar situation with Amazon or any other company?
How are such cases typically handled during onboarding?
How to delete my Amazon Job account in India location ?
Is this something that usually gets resolved internally, or can it escalate further?

Do you guys think they might revoke my offer ? cause i have already resigned from my current company & my joining date is too near. (Amazonians please respond).

Thanks in advance!

title: What to do to get a job as quickly as possible in this market?
content:
Hi all, I have 4.5 yoe and was laid off this January. I took February and half of March mostly preparing DSA and LLD/HLD, then started aggressively applying mid March, got 4-5 interview callbacks through April, got rejected in them with mixed feedback. Now my savings are running out, my resume gap is increasing beyond 3 months, and I need a job as soon as possible.

For DSA, I solve sheets, give contests (currently rated ~1600) and use zerotrac.
For LLD, I haven't done much but I use algomaster.io which is by the guy who made the awesome low level design github repo (ashishps1).
For HLD, I use Hello Interview - have covered the basics, just do one question a day in guided practice.
For behavioral, I might do Hello Interview's course too.

I currently apply via Instahyre, LinkedIn Jobs and naukri.

What all would you advise to consider or incorporate in my jobhunt strategy?

title: Need Advice: Choosing Between Kotak, WEX, Navan and Lucidity (YOE 5)
